reviews.review_text: We went up for a family weekend getaway. We had a lovely time using the pool, walking track along the Murray and the BMX track whilst we were there. The kids loved riding around the place too. There was a lot to entertain the kids

Great place to stay, school holidays aren't the best time as far as people, but the entertainment the park provides for that time is brilliant. Toilets and showers, people need to be more respectable.
Staff are sensational, good amenities and attractions and location. Great place to stay, we extended our time xx
Didn't stay. Too far out of town.  Lovely and clean. Caravan section next to the main road.
Good little park, more suited to families with young kids, great kids water park, alongside the Murray River, a short drive from Echuca CBD
Fantastic caravan park, very helpful staff
Nice big spacey park, amenities are very clean, plenty for the kids to do, an lots of walking trails by the river. We will be back for sure. Echuca has so much to see and do.

Short stay (3 nights) in unpowered section, which was super nice. Nature aspect, which we love. Lots of birdsong. Stunning view of the Murray.  Outdoor heated pool & amenities block was well appointed & maintained ato standard.

Disgusting and run down cabins.. Sent an email with the cabin we were in flaws and no response. They are negligent and I don't recommend staying here. Power switches were dangerous and asking for electrocution. emailed them twice no response. $400 a night.. get stuffed. Ow also nothing for adults do do no gym and only tennis court has tree root in middle of it..

Cons: Booked our sites nearly 6 months ago, when booking we picked a spot that could fit a big group of us (2 cabins & 8 vans), no-one had currently booked that area so we had the pick of it. Upon getting there we had been moved and not everyone together. Facilities were putrid, dirty, clumps of hair ect we had to ask for them to be cleaned. "Indoor" pool is very dated and falling apart.
Pros: Park is huge & very close to the murray, kids craft and movie was great.
Sorry to say we won't be staying again as the bad outweighed the good.

Wonderful park. Excellent location, quiet with no traffic noise and on the river. Facilities always clean. We enjoyed the location so much we extended twice. Site fees very low😊. Definitely recommend.

Great place to stay.
Although it's a little out of town, it has everything you need.
Well maintained Park

Lovely park!
Grounds are very well manicured, lovely clean amenities. Quite big sites.
We have a big rig and we had plenty of room.
Plenty of things for kids to do, heated pool, camp kitchen  walking track off the park.
Highly recommend 👍

Fantastic Park! Been coming here for 4 years and I think it gets better every year. We stayed over Easter and there was lots for the kids to do, jumping pillow, water park, BMX track, train ride around the park for the little ones. The egg throw in at the tennis courts was brilliant and the train the night before left the kids extremely happy. The park is really big so lots of space for kids to ride bikes. Amenities are clean. Location is great 5 min out of town and park runs a long the river, great for a long walk and cycling. Staff were lovely. Pizza food truck on site was very good. Can't wait for next year!

My daughter loved the facilities - the pool, jumping pillow and splash park. The little train topped off her best Easter experience. Management were super accommodating, we will be staying here again in the future

Stayed over the Easter weekend 2026, absolutely loved it these so much for the kids to do they will not get bored.
Staff are very patient and lovely, highly recommend this park for anyone with kids.

What an amazing place to stay. So much to do for kids of all ages.
We just spent Easter here and had the BEST time. Unfortunately our BBQ on our balcony didn’t work which threw our meal planning out, and our bed in the main bedroom was extremely hard but the feedback was received really well at checkout. We have already booked to come back next year. Thanks for such a wonderful long weekend the kids will remember it for a long time!
Great for families

Had a 2 night stay in a cabin. Very clean and roomy. Great for a family. Lots of cabins.
About a 5 minute drive from town centre.

Love this park and cannot wait to come  back!|We had a great time on our ensuite site over a few hot days in December!|3 POOLS ~ YES 3!!|Park was clean, so much for the kids to do, water park awesome, amenities clean. |Ensuite site was a ripper. Clean, tidy.

Check in was easy and the Check in person was nice. Cabin was ok, that's about it.
Apparently I was in a Superior Villa. It's a dated, family sized villa with a unwanted hair in the shower cubicle, a broken fly screen in one room and water temp and pressure that was abysmal. I've stayed in quite a few Discovery Park sites before but as a worker seeking overnight accommodation, this place I will be avoiding.
Oh, and if you're rather tall and a larger build, bring your own towels as the bath towels are some of the smallest and thinnest that I have ever used in accommodation.
I don't doubt that this place is great for kids, just not great for adults.
The WiFi, absolutely terrible if you're located further in to the park.
Great weekend accomodation for what I needed. Decor is a bit on the dated side in cabin 18 but clean and what I needed.

Just spent 4nights, were only going to stay 2. Great park with good facilities and amenties. Only negative is some of the powered sites were a bit tight. We opened our awning and we nearly touched the next van.

We stayed on a drive through site whichare small... the toilets were not clean and no hand soap and absolutely no internet and reception for TV was useless.. bring books and DVDs.. park very quiet and relaxing.. lots for young kids to enjoy so that's a positive.  But really with a beautiful park spend money on better reception for TV and don't offer free WiFi if you don't have it..

We absolutely loved our stay at Discovery Parks – Echuca and can honestly say it’s a fantastic destination for both families and adults. There is so much to enjoy in one place, with plenty of activities to keep the kids entertained, including a splash park, waterslides, indoor and outdoor pools, playgrounds, jumping pillows, and even a basketball court. It’s such a fun environment that you hardly need to leave the park — unless, of course, you’ve brought a boat or Jet Ski!
The staff were consistently warm, welcoming, and genuinely helpful, which made a wonderful first impression from the moment we arrived. The amenities were also very clean and well maintained.
What truly makes this park stand out is just how much there is to do. The kids will never be bored, with so many activities and spaces to explore. It creates a fun, lively atmosphere while still feeling relaxed and family-friendly.
We’re already looking forward to our next visit.

Could not recommend this place more! We love this park & continue to come back. Perfect for families. Great location. Great facilities at the park. Close to the river. Rooms were clean. The indoor heated pool & double waterslide were the big hits with all the family, young & old!! We continue to come back every year!

Looooved this place.   Quiet and located right on the Murray River.  We stayed in a small couple,  I think it was called a Standard cabin which was perfect in size for my husband and I and perfect price for our wallet.   After staying in a few budget motel rooms and then coming to this little cabin,  we basically decided to stay in discovery Park cabins for the remainder of our trip wherever possible.   Much, much better standard and more user friendly for want of a better term.   The air conditioner was quiet enough to sleep with if necessary.   The full kitchen was perfect for meals and cleaning up and the bathroom was modern, clean and a decent size for a small cabin.   Could have stayed forever.  You can walk along the Murray's River banks and watch the boats,  paddle streamers if your lucky.   Take in the wildlife if you like that and just relax in the peace and quiet.  If you get the chance to stay,  do it.  Perfect place to unwind.   Took photos after we unpacked so you'll see some of our gear in the unit.

Great park. Clean and tidy and well maintained. Easy walks along Murray River either walking or bike. Very helpful and friendly Staff. Many thanks to staff member Stephen (with a “PH”) for his help with an extension cord.

Well maintained park. Lots of activities for kids. Clean amenities.  Close proximity to river and boat ramps. Nice clean pool. Not enough shaded camp sites. Overall very good but only rated 4 star. This is based on comparison with discovery parks Lake Hume. 10 mins to town centre. Also worth a trip to town to see sand sculptures if still there. Nearby Rochester 30 mins away to view art murals.
We enjoyed our stay, it's a very clean park and got everything for everyone

just stayed here, it was relatively nice, close to town, cabin nice and clean. down side indoor pool a bit dirty along the walls and the wifi.. forget it.. its terrible they may aswell not even have it.

What a fantastic park! We just spent the Aus Day weekend. We opted for a unpowered site with our van (due to friends in roof top& tents) and I am so glad we did as the space on the unpowered site was amazing! And we had River access
Pools at every corner and of course the kids LOVED the splash park!
Thanks for a great weekend Discovery we will be back

We booked on line just after Christmas.The rates for this time of the year was very reasonable.When booking on line we weren't sure where we would have been placed,but to our surprise our site was perfectly placed to have shade all day. We decided to stay an extra night.
The office staff were very helpful and extremely polite. We come up to Echuca  few times  year and will definitely stay here again.
Thanks G'Day Parks.

Stayed Sunday- Saturday in drive through site. They are pretty narrow, neighbours awning nearly touching our van, wouldn't book one again just a standard site would fit our 24ft van easy enough. Sunday to Thursday park was pretty quiet and nice then got bust as to be expected Thursday into the weekend.

Park was great they had so many activities for the kids and staff fantastic.

Easy access to river for a swim as well.

We had a river side caravan site, was absolutely beautiful, this park is so well thought out! The variety of food trucks was amazing. The laundry facilities well thought out with even a money change machine in the laundry with card access aswell. Great sized sites. Easy access to the river. Just amazing .. I haven’t even mentioned the variety of different pools around the park or the awesome water play park …. We will be back one day … wish we had of booked a longer stay
Great fun park for kids

Stayed in a cabin very close to the main area of the park, my partners been staying in the same cabin for over 10 years.  The park is lovely, great family park, clean and great location on the Murray.
However, for the price of the cabin the services provided are poor.  We were booked for. A week and each year we get TWO dishwashing tablets, a couple of rubbish bags, minimal tea and coffee  and only one set of clean towels for the whole stay.  For the price we pay over $3K for a week you would expect enough to last the week.
Other than those few issues a great family park.

We tented here on a powered site. Well portioned areas to set up. The facilities were like new and were clean and well maintained. Right on the Murray River and easy access to Echuca. The park facilities were excellent for young kids including the water park, 3 pools, bouncing pillow and playgrounds. Would happily stay there again.

Ensuite site awesome, and SOOOO Much for the kids to do!||The ensuite site was spotless! There was also shampoo and body wash and body lotion !! Very impressed to see this in a caravan park! There is no shower caddy, so BYO if you do not want to leave items on the shower floor. ||Camp kitchen huge, clean,  and nice to be able to either sit inside or outside on a nice night. ||The splash park for the kids is AMAZING!! Great for kids of all ages!! So much fun!! And 3 pools!! 3!!! We were lucky enough to get perfect weather so tested out all 3!||We thoroughly enjoyed our stay and cannot wait until we return!!
Very impressed by these Caravan Park. Has gantastic modern  facillites. Camp kitchen had airconditioning and everthing you wouls want|Locatwd on the Murray just out of town
Fantastic.

Unfortunately had to cancel our trip due to unforeseen circumstances. However when speaking to reception, I was met with the coldest, most rude receptionist, totally blunt and uninterested. We were going to rebook in the new year as we do family trips to big 4 and discovery parks often with our children, we won’t be coming here though.
Very nice holiday park just next to river. Cabins were spacious and clean.
Park is clean, amenities clean that's top priority.  No dogs allowed. Good thing dont want barking dogs all night.
We were in our van. But from checking in the service was wonderful and friendly and easy
Great park can take up to 12m long

We stayed with our caravan on a powered drive through site earlier this week for two nights.
The park and all facilities are clean tidy and well kept. The Murray is close by. A trip into Echuca is a 5 minute drive.
Excellent park.

The park itself is mostly worthy of 5 stars, but loses a few stars because:
- it took us 5 or 6 attempts over 2 days to call before someone answered,
- the woman who answered was rude, abrupt, very unhelpful and somewhat misleading,
- we were assigned a totally unsuitable site,
- park managers don't seem to care,
- a group of young girls playing loud music, shouting and generally being nuisances weren't dealt with,
- a dog wandering off lead was reported and we were told it's probably just one of the locals' dogs (we booked here coz it's a no-dogs park!)
So overall, it's ok but not great.
Would we stay here again? Hmm, tricky, really not sure.

Reception staff member was extremely rude and a very unhelpful.
These days not to be a pet friendly park is totally unacceptable, but when someone enquires you could be a bit friendlier with your response.
Would not recommend just for the attitude.

The park advertises that they provide free wifi but it apparently only works intermittently and we had no access for the duration of our stay. This affected our phones, computers and made the smart TV virtually useless. ||We booked for 4 but bedding was only set up for 2. Otherwise the cabin was quite good and located next to the water park and covered pool which was great.
Out of town slightly, huge park, lots of space to wander, brilliant facilities for families with numerous pools, BBQs and great caravan feel.
One of the better parks we have stayed at and there have been a lot of them in the last 18 months
Had everything you need for a holiday park!!

It out of town, and quiet.

Did I mention it's quiet

Fabulous quiet park a bit out of town !
I'm so pleased we found this park. It was actually the price that caught our attention to begin with  as it was much,much cheaper than all the others.
Once we arrived we were amazed at the great facilities available.
Just to name only some of the things available were the indoor pool ,out door pool, splash park, , jumping pillow, bike park, walking track along the river, undercover bbq areas and plenty of clean toilets and showers dotted all through the park.
All staff were extremely friendly and helpful in person and on the phone.
We will definitely be staying here each time we visit Echuca from now on.
The park is very nice. Our cabin was very clean, & the bed was great. Park amenities were above average as well. Just don't turn up at 4:55pm. The reception staff were a bit grumpy. 🙂

Park was well kept, heaps to do for the kids. Admin wouldn't allow a 2nd car inside even though the park was pretty empty. Had to leave bang on 10 because there were people booked. A friend who stayed an extra night send a video of the 4 sites we occupied empty, as well as the other 8 that were empty the whole 4 days we were there. Felt a bit deceitful. Still, the park itself was really nice.
Stayed in an ensuite site. Lovely van park, well layed out, ensuite sites are very level. Will definitely be back. Splash park also looked great for a hot day also. Quiet location.

Park is situated out of main town and near river park. Variety of sites including grass, gravel, ensuite. My site was well designed with angle gravel parking site for caravan which makes reversing super easy, plus  large mat area surrounded by grass verges. The amenities were clean and well maintained although there may be a queue when the park is full over the summer months. This is a large park with one way roads, pools, water park, cabins and permanent sites. Easy drive to town and no road noise. A great park.
We left our step behind we emailed them still waiting to hear back?,  a good park  enjoyed our stay.
Absolutely beautiful cabins that are clean and family friendly! Love the spacious and natural surroundings. Kids love the water park and swimming area!
Pool could have been much warmer

What a Brilliant park, Well kept and maintained , the Facilities are absolutely first class , and the Camp Kitchen near our Site was First Class ( Full Stainless Steel Appliances) two 5burner/oven , two S/S fridges , microwave , and indoor/ outdoor BBQ all surrounded by outdoor dining +indoor dining settings “ TV too

Absolutely perfect spot. We needed an overnight campervan spot with ensuite. Just fabulous. Great price.
Staff were lovely.  Hoping to stop again ☺️

We chose to stay here for a 3 night get away.
Couldn’t have been happier. Beautiful 3 bedroom cabin overlooking the river. Spacious lounge/ dining/ kitchen. Wonderful large decking area with a small bbq. As the weather was cool, we were able to enjoy the heated indoor pool.
Beautiful to wake up to the sounds of kookaburras in the morning.

Travelling around Australia and stayed in Echuca discovery park. So far one the best stays we've had. Staff were exceptional cabin was second to none excellent value for for money. Thanks for a wonderful stay. Much appreciated Jeff and Liv.
The best caravan park that I have visited in 2 months
Amazing staff facilities stayed longer beautiful location booked for 3 nights stayed 5 can't wait to get back here
Would of given 5 stars but 12 bucks to dry your clothes was over the top . Most I have ever paid

Very clean and newish facilities at this well appointed cabin / caravan park. We stayed a very cold weekend during July and appreciated the heating in the communal toilets and showers. Caravan sites were level and had matting to minimise mud and debri. Only complaints were too few bins and the security touch pads at the entry and exits were too low.
Very clean and tidy park. Great amenities. The kids had a blast on the jumping pillow swimming in the indoor pool.

Beautiful park! Lovely and clean.|We stayed in a waterpark view 2 bed unit, it was lovely inside, comfortable bed and pillows - air-conditioned and fan in the main room. Doors blocked out most of the noise, waterpark and indoor pool just steps away.||Also has mini golf and dirt box track, as well as muddy walking trails- was a food and coffee van on site and some holiday activities.||It is a decent drive from town but is peaceful and quiet, reception is spotty.|We have since moved to a different park in moama and wish we had stayed! The best park in the area.

A really amazing park with all the good stuff. Fantastic and clean camp kitchen, heated pool, superb amenities and huge sites. Reason is only gave 4 stars is lack of bins around park and the height of the entry and exit code boxes. Please raise them so I can reach them

Spent 11 days in this G’Day Park and enjoyed every minute.
Situated on the mighty Murray and a short drive into the town centre, the parks location is very convenient.
We opted to catch a cab into town which was also a convenient way to go.
We hired an ensuite site, which was appreciated when the weather turned a little grey. The ensuite was modern and clean, with bath mat and hand towel provided.
The park has plenty to keep the kids occupied as well.
We ate at the 1895 Royal Hotel before walking to the historic port precinct to cruise on the Murray.
Enjoyed our stay and recommend this park to those traveling to Echuca.

Place was good as it sits beside the murray river and walkable distance, but thr accomodation was no good as the accomodation was attached to other accomodations and people can walk through and steal if the doors are not locked properly. Washroom was not cleaned properly and can see the shit still. Also the pillow covers was not cleaned properly. Internet is worst. No matter it says free wifi and password provided but doesnt work at all. Entertainment room was closed before the time mentioned.

Very clean and comfortable
Highly recommended

We stayed here on the long weekend  and  no room service, no proper heating, rooms weren't  cleaned properly,  internet needs up grading, cos u don't get reception .with all the money they would get u would think they would upgrade it all.no information folder .sorry I had to write a review like this .
7nights on powered sites. Great place plenty to do around town

Loved our stay, the cabins were amazing, spaciuos, clean and located very well. Kids had a great time with the jumping pillow, the indoor pool and even the splash park. Loved the activity book guven to the kids, a nice little touch. Friendly service and lovely surroundings. Can't wait to come back.

Very clean and well laid out with a large variety of options to suit most budgets. The waterpark located next to the indoor heated pool was the kids favourite. They used the cold water park and then jumped in the heated pool, good for adults as well. 5 mins from town.
Very nice park good walks along river
Beautiful surroundings, lovely staff, spotlessly clean, beautiful new cabins. We stay here often, and it has gotten so much better ♥️

Pro’s for us:

- Excellent Pool Facilities
- Amazing Jumping Pillow
- Wonderful Water Park
- Awesome Daily Entertainment
- Clean, Maintained, Van Sites
- Felt Safe.
- Water Pressure A1.
- Shower was Hot

Con’s for us:

- 300 + steps return from Van site (9) to nearest bathroom to door of Van (the kids had fun counting I guess), extreme problematic, not relaxing when you have 2+ children requiring escort at various times of the night and day to use facilities, would be a nightmare in winter and I felt like I was disturbing others when treading 8 feet over the stones at 2 am to use the toilet.

- Nearest toilet to Van site closed when water park closes.

- Water Park Toilets, Smelly, No Soap, No Toilet Paper on 2 of the 3 days we stayed.

- Dryer requires an upgrade as it simply doesn’t dry clothes, I recommend the 24/7 laundromat 5 min drive away so you’re not slaving over washing.

- Machine to the Left swallows coins, (was refunded).

- One Single Key for access to bathrooms despite paying for the total of 5 people to stay, this meant scheduling our showers / toilet stops at the same time.

- No soap holders in Bathroom for men’s facilities.

- Shower Heads need replacing in female toilet.

- One Single Gate Access Code which meant having to attend front car park for stocked refrigerated items. (Despite there being plenty of space available)

Indifferent:
- Mini golf course was fun, but is run down.

We would absolutely stay here again, a en-suite site is a necessity, for those that don’t have the ability to be self contained in the van. Not sure if there is a dump point?
The kids had an amazing time!!

We had a wonderful stay over the Easter weekend. The welcome Easter pack with the activity books was a sign of the care and attention staff provide to the park and its visitors. The park has incredible facilities, the splash park was a huge hit, the music and food trucks were a bonus. There Easter kid activities from colouring in competitions, to the Easter egg games. Facilities were clean, tidy, sites were big and well maintained. Couldn’t ask for more.

Very nice cabins and facilities.
The pools are great.
Wifi is crap till after midnight.
And telstra and optus 5G is crap here as well

Absolutely amazing park to stay at.|We had a 2 bedroom cabin that overlooked the bush and Murray River from the spacious balcony.|The facilities are brilliant.|Outdoor pool, indoor pool and a water park that was fantastic fun.|There is a games room and BBQ sites dotted all over the park.|This is a fantastic place to stay and the staff were all lovely and friendly.|The caravan sites also looked great.

Absolutely in the Top 3 parks I've travelled between here and Cairns likely my no.1 it has everything space quiet tranquillity the Best camp kitchens the Best games room indoor heated pool outdoor pool also massive undercover jumping pillow bmx track mini golf riverside ride walk sit enjoy.
BBQ everywhere bicycle repair station friendly staff. Great for all ages.
HIGHLY RECOMMEND OVER SNY OTHER PARKS IN THE AREA.
Follow us @big_rig_travelling insta for great videos of the park.

We camped in our camper for $22
The camp kitchen was almost new and great. It's a shame people are dirty.
The toilets are great
Recommended
Clean and comfortable ideal.
We stayed in our caravan and just love the place.
Very quiet

It is nice and comfortable their. Can cook your own food. Or 10 minute drive to town center.  My cabin was very clean.  Only down side no information in the cabin of local food joints that could deliver food as reception was closed to ask as I had no transport to get to town center.

I'd probably be back in a few weeks.

Great spot near the river.
Amenities clean and well priced.
Great park with a pool

**Greenhaven Park Review: 10 Lines**
1. A serene urban escape with lush lawns, shaded trails, and a picturesque central lake teeming with wildlife.
2. Perfect for jogging, kayaking (rentals available!), or lazy picnics under century-old trees.
3. Family-friendly with a modern playground, clean restrooms, and plenty of picnic spots.
4. Dog owners rejoice—there’s a fenced “Bark Meadow” 🐾 for off-leash fun.
5. Wheelchair-accessible paths and a sensory garden ensure inclusivity for all visitors.
6. Seasonal magic: cherry blossoms in spring, fiery foliage in fall, frosty tranquility in winter.
7. Limited parking on weekends—arrive early to snag a spot.
8. Lacks enough benches for tired walkers, but trails are well-maintained.
9. Don’t miss the lively farmers’ market (summer weekends) and outdoor concerts!
Huge park. Has everything. Kids loved the splash park. No busy road noise. 3 bed cabin was generous in size.

I stayed the the superior cabin. It has absolutely everything and it’s all new. The towels are big and fluffy. It lost 2 stars due to terrible phone reception and NO WIFI. I couldn’t watch Netflix. Everything else is great. But the floors were absolutely filthy. The containers for the coffee and tea have visible dust in it. Not sure what the cleaners are doing. Yet if you don’t wash up and put things away, YOU get charged a cleaning fee. When they don’t clean anything.
The lady on reception is lovely
The front desk staff were helpful and friendly despite the fact that we had troubles on our travel  but the desk staff should be complimented

Stayed here on Australia Day weekend had a great time, it was our second trip in our new Austrack Talawana X15 hybrid off road van & we had a great time we stayed on site 10 overlooking the Murray River watching the goings on, up & down the river as well as having the luxury of toilets & showers only steps away from us.Echuca has so much to offer & see should you want to get about & see what echuca is on show. I rate the Discovery Park & Echuca a resounding 15 out of 10, it's a great place to visit.👍😊
Excellent amenities close to the river had a wonderful stay
Really nice place as was fully packed. Great stuff for kids to do like jumping pillow water slides and pool

- Terrible phone service/wifi (couldn’t even send and receive calls/text messages while at the park)
- The ammenities are not evenly distributed throughout the park. Our site was so far from any of the ammenities blocks and we had young children in a caravan with no bathroom that needed to be taken to the bathroom several times throughout the night. It is really not good enough, for the size of the park they definitely need another ammenities block closer to the side with the splash park to accommodate the sites closer to there.
- The park run out of toilet paper and wasn’t replaced for half the day. We went multiple times to the bathrooms throughout the day and the toilet paper had still not been replaced.
- The ammenities ran out of hot water and people were having cold showers (in the morning when it is the busiest time for showering) was still no hot water half way through the day.
- Also no fault of the parks but was one of the worst experiences we’ve had with other park patrons. It was soo busy and there were so many entitled rude people with no respect for anyone else. We saw a lot of fighting between children and adults around the splash park/pool area and the kids jumping pillow and pump track area.
- Splash park area and toilets constantly smelled of urine.

Only pros were the site we had was very spacious (although no shade) and there was a lot for the kids to do to keep busy.

Overall wouldn’t be rushing back.
Definitely need ammenities added closer to the caravan sites nearest to the splash park area. Worst experience with this we’ve ever had.
Love this place, no need to go any where or spend any thing.
A large park next to the river.

Out of all the discovery park I dislike this one the most. I have no problems with internet at other  caravan parks but with this place I struggle to browse anything on my phone. Poor network coverage. The cabins are old and aren't clean. Hardly anything in the kitchen used for cooking, Overall it is okay to stay at but it would be my last option.
Great leafy park by the river. I would recommend this park.

It was honestly a great experience, with a great view of the river. The rooms were also nice and clean with comfy pillows and blankets. In the cabin I stayed at there were 2 bunk beds and one double size bed in a 2 room cabin. Along with this in the kitchen there were plates and other utensils like toasters, as well as a hair dryer and other things like soap and lotion provided. Not only this there were 2 TVs one in the living room and the other in the room with the double size bed. And there was easy access to the water activities to the cabin. So overall this is a great place to stay however not much to do in Echuca other than visiting the museum at the port and horse riding at the Billybong ranch, and other river activity like boating and hiking so if you come it will be a great place to just sit and relax and enjoy the view

Hope this helps anyone booking
Kids summer paradise ...no need to go to the gold coast ..come to Echuca instead ..3.5 drive from Melbourne, warm weather, not so humid and great value 😎

Easy to find and well located, clean park, reasonably well appointed for a family stay, pool (not warm), kids play area with Splash Zone, easy parking.|Cabin was clean and well prepared, ample linen all nicely presented, good shower with unlimited hot water.||This is a self catering site, there is no bar or restaurant on site, reception sells VERY basic things, however in the town (5 minutes drive) are a good range of shops and supermarket.||The Cabin is equipped with a reasonable kitchen, gas cooker with oven, microwave, family size fridge freezer and kettle.  There is sufficient kitchenalia to prepare meals.  Main bed is comfortable and with ample pillows, bunks are really only suitable for children and more in a corridor than a room.||Aircon in main room.||If you compared this to a large caravan it’s very comparable.||Internet is not dependable.

Office ladies not the friendliest!    Powered sites were great...however just double check bookings before arrival.  We booked online months before our friends how booked by phone. Our sites were split and when questioned was told ours was an unsecured booking (deposit paid).  We were put on a site that had been cancelled...had they not cancelled We wouldn't hv a
Had a site.

Disable amenities are within regular shower block...wheelchair access not possible and door was very heavy for person on crutches.  Also not being a stand alone amenities it meant carer couldn't enter if they weren't same sex.

Pretty disappointing, to be honest. Wifi is useless, spa dirty, DVD player broken, limited hot water.

We packed up at 430am and left to return home.
Located just 7 minutes out of town , clean facilities indoor heated pool, outdoor water park , river access/views kids love coming here
The bunk beds were very unstable. The ladder was missing a screw. The bed was missing 3 screw. Rooms were very small and crowded.
Very clean and well maintained. The pool being indoors made it very warm for the colder days. Our son loved it!

Well no hot water in main shower block for a week, terrible WiFi on Telstra, and when a group of us planned for a games night of billiards and table tennis the room was shut down at 8.00 on a Friday night, even in daylight saving. Not sure why they are opposed to people having fun on a holiday? Anyway overall lay out of park was great and amenities such as camp kitchen was well provided. Just need a little more attention to detail

We booked our cabin 9 months in advance of our trip with 2 nights in Echuca. We advised the park in our booking that we would be arriving late as we were driving from north of Sydney. The cabin we were given was right at the boom gate which was the worst cabin location in the park! Looking around the next day there were numerous empty similar cabins in much better locations.On entering the cabin it did not smell at all pleasant. We then faced having to make the children’s beds, when you pay for accommodation they should be made especially when you advise you will be late. We then found the mattress protectors to be absolutely filthy and urine stained so we took them off and made beds. At this location there is flood lighting and the poor fitting blinds do not block this out. Cleaning of cabin was not thorough with MM’s along edge of floor. The sofa was also rather grotty needing a deep clean.  The cabin itself has not been maintained well with a wall and door patched very unprofessionally. We were also woken both nights by hooning young people we were surprised on-site staff did not hear. I have emailed both park and Discovery days ago with no acknowledgment received. We will not renew our Discovery membership. Also the parks wifi was absolutely useless not good when Telstra rececption is patchy.
Great school holidays place. Heated pool and waterpark were very popular with the grandkids, ages 12 and 10. We did a river walk each day which was fun.
No wifi but it was advertised.  Drive through site was excellent
Quite an amazing place to when camping        I went their Friday to Sunday it's been amazing with all the things the pool, the park

Great location
Accommodation not so great. My eldest didn't want to sleep in the bedroom. The Accommodation smelt and was not very clean.
The wifi ..there was none.. I booked it for the kitchen and wifi but this did not work. There was hardly any reception. That needs to be taken off the website.
The kids room was tiny. We were right at front and the constant traffic of cars meant we needed to keep the blinds down and windows closed. Not sure we were compensated.
The location is perfect..near the river. Great walks.
I paid $330 per night. I would have liked two of what everyone else got.

We have just returned from a 5 night stay at your Discovery Park in Echuca and we were very pleased with our stay.
We had an ensuite caravan site which was close to the River.
The park has a lot to offer including:
- outdoor pool (didn’t use though)
- indoor heated pool
- splash park with two water slides
- mini golf
- games room including some PS2s
- theatre room
- two playgrounds
- BMX track
- jumping pillow
- coffee van
- food van
- tennis court
- basketball
- camp kitchen

It’s great to see that this park is a dog free park (my daughter is afraid of dogs and this becomes problematic when holidaying).

There is all types of accommodation available such as powered sites, cabins, ensuite sites.

There were also children’s activities everyday of the school holidays including a daily colouring competition (my daughter was very excited to win a prize). There were also art and craft all free of charge.

There is really so much to do in the park that you don’t have to leave.

The only issues we had were the limited wifi,
but we were told of this when we arrived, and also our annex pegs wouldn’t drill into the ground due to the hard rocky surface underneath.
Great stay none the less.
A spacious well maintained park. Lovely amenities.  Heated pool is huge. Staff are friendly and helpful. We stayed for 5 days .Highly recommend and would definitely stay again.

Not a fan - most expensive park we have been to yet and no wifi or ph reception. Large park, clean facilities and very friendly staff however we did pull up to do some work online and couldn’t which was disappointing.

The Deluxe river view cabins are great in size and amenities but was expecting the second bedroom to have one bunk bed with a double bed on bottom and a single on top as shown on website but instead got two bunks consisting of four single beds which made it difficult for tall adults to use and the pillows kept falling down the gap between the bunk and wall while you were sleeping .TV reception kept dropping out and had to keep rescanning to get the channels back and wifi was very weak and often not working at all.
3 stars kids were so excited for the spa, and the spa did not work. jumping Castle went down at 5pm the next day, we went to the water park was broken-down it was a good experience tho.
Great place the cabin was excellent close to Echuca centre and surrounds.
Nice place
2nd time we have stayed here & we enjoyed both times. Park is still in the process of restoration & renewal. Not far out of town right next to the river.

Fair enough stay,great location with Murray River a 2min walk,visiting wildlife on yr doorstep. Quiet,clean.
Shame the spa was out of order in Superior spa suite & beds downsized to 2 instead of 4 bunkbeds advertised, with no notice.
Booked a powered ensuite site which was clean and well presented.
Great caravan park and the price for a night $37.80 drive through site with power and water would be good value except for no hot water for the morning shower 🥶

- Inefficient wi fi & phone coverage.
- Despite piling oodles of money into the laundry - inefficient dryers. Clothes had to dry out for DAYS in our cabin. Despite being made aware of this,  management offered no reimbursement at all - indifferent. $4 for a cold wash overpriced.
- Gas cooking facilities in the cabins sub standard.
- Had to make own beds with linen provided.

- Staff basically indifferent to all of the above.

Not worth the price at all.

Just back over a week ago from a stay at your park. We booked a powered site with Ensuite (Ensuite 26 to be precise) and had originally only planned to stay for 2 nights. We enjoyed Echuca and your park so much we extended for an extra 2 nights. Reception ladies were very friendly and helpful and everything was very clean. Rates were great and we would recommend your park to anyone who wants a pleasant stay - we will be back. Thank you for making our stay great 👍

We camped on a power site for 4 nights we were given a grass sight next to the new camp kitchen when there was sun shine the building kept us in the shade might be good in summer not fun in winter. It was lovely using the new amenities to go in and the heater was running very nice. The camp kitchen is wonderful with two Hugh ovens. Tried the heated pool to cold for us but it was June should not expect to much

Booked an ensuite caravan site for a couple of nights. The site is great. The ensuite is probably the nicest I have booked so far. Very clean and new and fresh. Very happy with the ensuite. The site is lovely too. Plenty of room for van and car and we are not too close to others. Park itself is lovely. Right on the river. Highly recommend.

Best caravan park I have ever stayed at.
Beautiful designed layout ,caravan bays were awesome 👌
Amenities 10 out of 10.
Just hope the price doesn't sky rocket.
I'll be back!!!
Good basic caravan park. Cheaper than the others.
Great location
Excellent Caravan Park

We stayed on the Anzac Long weekend so the park was very full. Nice location and also quiet despite the crowded park. Nice on site takeaway food van. good amenities for kids also
Telstra signal was 1 bar on 5G and was very disappointing making using the internet impossible despite being only 3kms as the Crow flies from the town centre. I understand they are getting star link and I hope this helps.
The laundry was a great disappointment as you could not do your washing with hot water (disabled) and the driers on the hot setting was non existent. (We had three concurrent sessions with the drier and still had to hang up our washing to dry. Our neighbours in the park all talked negatively about the driers and the washing machines.
Best campsite amenities I have ever seen! Clean, modern and lots for families to do

We had an ensuite site for 3 nights.
Reasonably priced at $55/night for 3 adults.
This was during school holidays.
Many facilities for children and very large park. Looks like they are expanding too.

First stay at this park. Great facilities for the kids, excellent to have indoor heated pool and. basketball hoops. Lovely large sites and beautiful natural environment. Lovely friendly staff who were very accommodating too.
Great place great staff been coming here for 18 years

The staff are absolutely amazing!!! We’ve stayed at a lot of Discovery Parks but this one is by far our families favourite. On check in the kids were given show bags with activities to do including a plate painting kit. The park put on a great Easter morning full of Cadbury treats. My son celebrated his first birthday here and the wonderful team gifted him the cutest plush teddy. Only downside is very limited reception but there are DVDs to borrow in case the kids want some down time from the pools, waterpark, BMX track and jumping pillow. The staff are so attentive, they are on top of everything.
This is our first time staying at this park. Sites are amazing, staff are friendly and helpful. Amenities are awesome!! A great place to be! Quiet! We’ll be back!!
Was a beautiful place to stay evething you need for kids, plenty of room on there camp sites.
Stayed with eminities block.  So very clean, beautiful modern and clean camp kitchen.  Great prices.  Highly recommend for a great stay.
It's not dog friendly even though maps says it is. I hate this place!

Wonderful Team here that always provide a professional consistent service .

The cabin is fantastic with all you need . A great location where you can relax in tranquillity and unwind . I travel for business and the cabin is ideal . The local wildlife and kookaburras are a highlight after a long day . Thanks team

Support local businesses

Highly recommend.
Interior and appliances need an upgrade in some cabins. Water park fantastic for the kids.

The splash park was a huge hit with the kids and the adults the out door pool was good until it got packed out, the gate on the jump pillow was broken and not enough shade, the river room cabin was really old just needs a bit of cash splashed on it and I think it would have been perfect, new beds and a new couch would have made a whole lot of difference! We also couldn’t get phone reception anywhere in the park and the free wifi wouldn’t work anywhere in our cabin a

We had a wonderful 2 nights here.
Location was great to explore Echuca and surrounding areas.
The toilets showers being cleaned.  They just put in any time they want too . The toilets were shut for longer than the times on back off the door .
A great park with lots of activities for kids. The service is amazing and the staff welcoming. We were a big group and all of us had such a good time.
Great place to stay amazing park heaps of stuff to keep the kids entertained
Really lovely little river view cabin! Great value- the cabin had one king bed and 8 bunkbeds. Large kitchen, comfy couch and quiet at night. Wifi- not so good.

A very tidy, clean and pleasant cabin. A comfortable bed & good shower was wonderful after our 9 hour drive to Echuca. There wasn't much of a river view but a short walk takes you to its banks. Good for a single night stay.

Just spent a family weekend at Echuca. Park was amazing kids had an absolute blast. Rooms were very clean and modern staff were very helpful and friendly splash park was a big hit with the kids. Highly recommended
A bit tight in the park. No good for larger vans unless you go through drive through. Recovering from the floods so everything is new

Close to Murray River and amenities very good.
Developing more cabins so will be even better in future.
Great park, plenty to keep the kids entertained. Cabin was clean and perfect size.
We had a problem during the early night that could have waited till morning but  tge Chap went out of his way to help us.

Great van park and cabins galore. Staff are very welcoming and helpful.
It has been greatly appreciated that the TV rooms are open for the AFL lead up to finals playing currently...thanks very much management 😀😀 will definitely return to see the new facilities and parking spots.

Wow.  Our family had the best time at Discovery Echuca. Spacious cabins,  activities everywhere for the kids and big kids in a beautiful fun tree setting by the Murray.  What more could you ask for.  We will be back.
Have been going with my dad for a long been the best experience every single time love the people and love the location
This is the very best park I have visited , clean great amenities ,staff very friendly and helpful.such a pity the floods did so much damage , great to see  up and running

Nice place and Quiet place
River view rooms
Clean rooms/ Utensils
Cosy rooms
TV/DVD Units
Comfortable beds/ enough quilts
Lot of fun activities like heated pool/Indoor Game room/ Tennis court/ Water Theme park/ Jumping jack
Coffee shop inside the park
Secured Gated place

Disadvantage: For me being a techie
-Horrible internet coverage ( Free wifi)
-Optus/ Telstra service is also limited
-A very limited tissues/ Bathroom supplies
-No Washing oil/ Dryer
Caravan sites were a little muddy but all things considered with the floods & rain it was still a very nice stay right by the Murray.
Short walk from the river
What a great little park, with plenty of stuff for the kids to do. With a great location 👌
We booked a large caravan site and got a small one NOT happy they had lots of big spots so yes we won't extend our stay had to use another site to put our car on!

After booking this site we asked for a large site instead only got a tiny site also our neighbours here paid $22 per nite we paided alot more not happy this has happened twice with discovery caravan parks in one week be careful when booking!!
